procedural_signals.evidence: Agent's message listed 3 separate issues (freezer at Primrose, hot water at 829 OP, range hood at 829 OP) plus meta-commentary about same tenant/unit. Jose ignored the whole message and only engaged on the simpler one-at-a-time follow-ups (CO alarm, blinds).

notes: Darwin was retired this session (Jose said they no longer use him). Darwin entity row deleted from DB. Kept in S5 gold as expected_entity because he was mentioned in chat.
Two 11th St addresses in one session (824 + 828) is the worst extractor disambiguation case so far. Extractor would need to look at unit number context to split them.
Cross Appliance + Home Improvement Services were suggested by the agent for the ignored issues. Both are real vendors in legacy; both belong in expected_entities even though they weren't picked.

topic: No hot water at 1023 17th St → Jose handles via JL

expected_issues.summary: No hot water at 17th St
expected_issues.notes: Andrew (the agent operator) flagged that Abraham already had 5 open work orders before suggesting him. Jose chose JL — meaning Jose handled the work himself.

extractor_corrections.extractor_picked: merged into existing '15th street' informal entity from S2
extractor_corrections.reason: CRITICAL VECTOR-MATCH BUG. The LLM emitted 'property: 17th street'. resolveEntity step 2 (vector match) compared 'property:17th street' embedding against existing entities of kind=property and found '15th street' (informal, created in S2) above the 0.72 similarity floor. Result: a new property entity was NOT created; '17th street' got falsely merged into '15th street'. Short street-name fragments produce near-identical embeddings.
fix_directions: Raise SIMILARITY_FLOOR for short names (length < N tokens).
Disable vector merge for informal-to-informal short-name matches.
Best fix is upstream: pass full address to extractor (Andrew's already addressing this in chat-side messages going forward).

notes: Vendor list: Andrew suggested Abraham despite noting his 5-open-WO backlog; Jose said he'd handle it himself via JL. Both are relevant entities for this session.
This is the first concrete case of the vector-match bug. Worth a regression test once we have the eval scorer in PR2.

topic: 50 Rose dishwasher motherboard + 17th St hot water followup

expected_issues.summary: Dishwasher motherboard failing at 50 Rose
expected_issues.notes: Kori inspected and diagnosed; Waadt Appliance to give replacement estimates. Tenant wants stainless to match fridge; owner requested estimates for BOTH white and stainless — owner has approval/preference signal.

expected_issues.summary: No hot water at 17th St — CONFIRMED FIXED
expected_issues.notes: Followup to S6. Tenant asked to test and report back if it recurs.

extractor_corrections.correction: 'Waad appliance' and 'Waadt' are the SAME vendor (Waadt Appliance)
extractor_corrections.extractor_picked: Two separate entities — 'Waad appliance' (informal) and 'Waadt Appliance' (legacy)
extractor_corrections.reason: Same vendor with two chat spellings. ILIKE didn't match 'Waad appliance' to 'Waadt Appliance' (because Waad ≠ Waadt). Vector similarity at 0.72 floor wasn't enough to merge them. This is the canonical Waad/Waadt dedup case.
fix_directions: Per-kind similarity tuning for vendor names (allow misspellings to merge harder).
Levenshtein distance fallback for short vendor names within the same workspace.
Or: trust legacy `vendors` table fuzzy-match first, only create informal if absolutely no near-match exists.

extractor_corrections.extractor_picked: merged into '15th street' informal entity (same bug as S6)
extractor_corrections.reason: Second occurrence of the short-street-name vector-match bug. Confirms it's reproducible.

procedural_signals.signal: Owner approval gate on appliance replacements
procedural_signals.evidence: Tenant wanted stainless, but owner asked for both white + stainless estimates before deciding. Indicates owner-side gate for non-trivial costs.
procedural_signals.derived_rule_candidate: For appliance replacements >$N or non-emergency, route owner approval before vendor commits.

notes: Unit 5 in the session summary was wrong — actual unit is 2. The LLM summary made a typo.
Two-vendor flow on one issue (Kori inspects → Waadt replaces). PR2/PR3 will need to handle vendor-handoff observation patterns.
Waad/Waadt dedup will need a one-off DB cleanup later (merge informal entity into legacy).

notes: Vendor-roster precedent refined: mentioned ≠ relevant. Only include vendors we actually use. Ismael excluded here; Dever included in S1 because Dever IS a vendor we use (just wasn't picked).
Two owner-approval gates in one session — owner approval is a more common pattern than initially apparent.

procedural_signals.evidence: User-confirmed: 'JL is a subset of Jose. Jose should be the entity node, and JL/Jose are the same.'
procedural_signals.derived_rule_candidate: Entity-extractor (PR2 onwards) should treat 'Jose' and 'JL' as aliases for the same entity (JL Unlimited Services LLC — the legacy vendor row). Going forward, when chat references 'Jose' as a vendor/handler, resolve to JL Unlimited Services LLC. When chat references 'Jose' as the PM (recipient of agent messages), it's the PM persona — out of scope for the entity layer.

procedural_signals.signal: PATTERN C — Jose contacts TENANT directly (distinct from JL dispatch and owner-coordination)
procedural_signals.evidence: S32: 'will call to discuss' = Jose calling tenant Ron about the flickering lights. NOT physical work, NOT owner-approval.
procedural_signals.derived_rule_candidate: Three Jose-behavior patterns to model: (A) JL dispatch — Jose does physical work [S13 homeless, S16 call box, S6 hot water]; (B) Owner coordination — Jose contacts owner for approval [S18]; (C) Tenant coordination — Jose calls tenant to discuss/manage expectations [S32]. Only Pattern A → JL entity; B and C are pure communication.

procedural_signals.signal: When a vendor previously failed at the same unit/issue, agent should suggest a different vendor before re-dispatching
procedural_signals.evidence: Osalpa tried at 1520 OP unit C in S24, found no issues. S32: same issue recurs, tenant explicitly asks for a different vendor.
procedural_signals.derived_rule_candidate: PR3 belief: 'Osalpa was unable to resolve flickering lights at 1520 Ocean Park unit C — try a different electrician for this recurrence.' Or general: 'For recurring issues, escalate to a different vendor.'

notes: This refines the JL rule significantly: not every 'I'll handle it' = JL dispatch. Must distinguish physical-work from communication.

Earlier gold entries (S13, S16) that added JL on 'Jose will handle' should be re-evaluated: was it physical work? If only communication, drop JL. Leaving them as-is for now and applying the refined rule going forward.
Oscar informal entity deleted from DB (id 879c8324). Osalpa is the canonical entity for that technician's work.
